subject: Chemistry
title: The Strong Nuclear Force
description: A short lesson to help you apply The Strong Nuclear Force.

hook.text: Protons are all positively charged. They should repel each other and blow the nucleus apart. Something holds them together.

buildup.text: Electromagnetic repulsion between protons is enormous at nuclear distances. A new force must overpower it.

discovery.text: The strong nuclear force is 100 times stronger than electromagnetism—but only works at distances under 10⁻¹⁵ meters.

twist.text: Neutrons help by adding strong force without electromagnetic repulsion. Too few neutrons and the nucleus flies apart.

climax.text: The strong force is carried by gluons, which bind quarks inside protons and neutrons. It's the universe's strongest force.

punchline.text: The strongest force in nature, invisible beyond a femtometer. It holds matter together.

quiz.question: Why don't the positively charged protons in a nucleus fly apart?
options: The strong nuclear force is 100 times stronger than electromagnetic repulsion at short range
Electrons between protons neutralize the charge
Neutrons create a magnetic shield around the protons
